name: delete_file
content: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt' occured durin delting file /Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt

I don't see a file named "test.txt" in your working directory. Please confirm if the file exists before attempting to delete it.

If you're sure, you can use the `rm` command to delete the file. Since it doesn't exist, you won't see any output.

```bash
rm /Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt
```

However, if you want to delete a non-existent file, you can use the following command with the `-f` (force) and `-i` (interactive) options:

```bash
rm -fi /Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt
```

This will prompt you to confirm whether you want to delete the file.

If you want to delete all files in the current directory, you can use the `rm` command with the `*` wildcard:

```bash
rm *
```
Hello! How can I assist you today?
could you tell me how to ssh with a key

To SSH with a key, you can use the following command:

```bash
ssh -i /path/to/your/key.pem username@hostname
```

Replace:

- `/path/to/your/key.pem` with the path to your private key file
- `username` with your username on the remote host
- `hostname` with the hostname or IP address of the remote host

Make sure the key file is in the correct format (.pem or .key) and that you've set the correct permissions on the key file:

```bash
chmod 400 /path/to/your/key.pem
```

Once you've entered the command, you should be logged in to the remote host without needing a password.
i want to clone a repo from github, how to do that

To clone a repository from GitHub, you can use the `git clone` command:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/user/repository.git
```

Replace:

- `https://github.com/user/repository.git` with the URL of the repository you want to clone, for example: `https://github.com/user/siri-examples.git`

This will download a copy of the repository to your local machine. The directory will be named after the repository.

Make sure you have Git installed on your machine. If you haven't already, you can install it by running:

```bash
brew install git
```

(on macOS)

You can then navigate into the cloned directory and start working on the repository:

```bash
cd repository.git
```
